question:
Suppose that a system is restored to "as good as new" periodically at intervals of time $T$. So the system renews itself at time $T, 2 T, \ldots$. Define system reliability under preventive maintenance (PM) as.

$$
R_M(t)=\operatorname{Pr}\{\text { failure has not occurred by time } \mathrm{t}\}
$$


In other words, the system survives to time $t$ if and only if it survives every PM cycle $\{1,2, \ldots, k\}$ and a further time $(t-k T)$.
(a) For a system with a failure time probability density function

$$
f(t)=\lambda^2 t e^{-\lambda t} \text { for } t>0, \lambda>0
$$

obtain the system reliability under PM and system mean time to first failure.
(b) Calculate the system mean time to first failure if PM is performed every 20 days and $\lambda=0.005$.
answer:
The system survives to time $t$ if and only if it survives every PM cycle $\{1,2, \ldots, k\}$ and a further time $(t-k T)$. Thus,

$$
R_M(t)=[R(T)]^k R(t-k T) \text { for } k T<t<(k+1) T \text { and } k=0,1,2, \ldots
$$


Interval containing $t$ Formula for $R_{\mathrm{M}}(t)$

$$
\begin{array}{ll}
0<t<T & R(t) \\
T<t<2 T & R(T) R(t-T) \\
2 T<t<3 T & {[R(T)]^2 R(t-2 T)} \\
\cdots & \\
k T<t<(k+1) T & {[R(T)]^k R(t-k T)}
\end{array}
$$


Thus, system reliability under PM is

$$
R_M(t)=[R(T)]^k R(t-k T) \text { for } k T<t<(k+1) T \text { and } k=0,1,2, \ldots
$$


Given the probability density function as from Eq. (7.1),
$$
f(t)=\lambda^2 t e^{-\lambda t} \text { for } t>0, \lambda>0
$$

then

$$
R(t)=\int_t^{\infty} f(x) d x=\int_t^{\infty} \lambda^2 x e^{-\lambda x} d x=(1+\lambda \mathrm{t}) e^{-\lambda t}
$$


Thus, from Eq. (7.4), we have

$$
\begin{aligned}
R_M(t) & =[R(T)]^k R(t-k T) \quad \text { for } k=0,1,2, \ldots \\
& =\left[(1+\lambda T) e^{-\lambda T}\right]^k\left[(1+\lambda(t-k T)) e^{-\lambda(t-k T)}\right]
\end{aligned}
$$


The system mean time to first failure (MTTFF) is

$$
\begin{aligned}
\text { MTTFF } & =\int_0^{\infty} R_M(t) d t \\
& =\int_0^T R(t) d t+\int_T^{2 T} R(T) R(t-T) d t+\int_{2 T}^{3 T}[R(T)]^2 R(t-2 T) d t+\ldots \\
& =\sum_{k=0}^{\infty}[R(T)]^k \int_0^T R(u) d u \\
& =\frac{\int_0^T R(u) d u}{1-R(T)}
\end{aligned}
$$
Thus,

$$
M T T F F=\frac{\int_0^T R(u) d u}{1-R(T)}
$$


Note that, from Eq. (7.5)

$$
\begin{aligned}
\int_0^T R(x) d x & =\int_0^T(1+\lambda x) e^{-\lambda x} d x \\
& =\frac{2}{\lambda}\left(1-e^{-\lambda T}\right)-T e^{-\lambda T}
\end{aligned}
$$


From Eq. (7.7), we have
$$
\begin{aligned}
&M T T F F=\frac{\int_0^T R(u) d u}{1-R(T)}=\frac{\frac{2}{\lambda}\left(1-e^{-\lambda T}\right)-T e^{-\lambda T}}{1-(1+\lambda T) e^{-\lambda T}}\\
&\text { (b) Here } T=20 \text { days, and } \lambda=0.005 \text { per day. }\\
&\text { MTTF } F=\frac{\frac{2}{0.005}\left(1-e^{-(0.005) 20}\right)-(20) e^{-(0.005) 20}}{1-(1+(0.005)(20)) e^{-(0.005)(20)}}=\frac{19.9683}{0.0047}=4,267.8 \mathrm{days} .
\end{aligned}
$$
